summary: Ukrainian forces reported elimination of enemy personnel near Temirivka and precise strikes on the R‑280 bridge near Chonhar, disrupting Russian logistics. Russia’s Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone‑production workshop in the Chuguev area, while a previously announced ballistic missile threat was cancelled. Additional Patriot missiles were sourced from expired stock and antidrone nets are slated for installation in Volyn.
key_points: Ukrainian units eliminated enemy personnel near Temirivka (DeepStateUA/23570).
Two precise strikes disabled the automotive bridge on the R‑280 route near Chonhar, affecting Russian supply lines (DeepStateUA/23569).
A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone‑production workshop in the Chuguev area (ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Ukraine obtained additional Patriot missiles from expired stock, transferring them to its armed forces (ukr_leaks_eng/30819).
A ballistic missile threat was called off, reducing immediate long‑range strike risk (kpszsu/64818).
Antidrone nets will be installed on roads in the Volyn region to protect against UAV attacks (ukr_leaks_eng/30834).
Four civilians were injured in an attack on a multi‑storey building in Pavlograd (UkraineNow/81833).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces eliminated enemy personnel near Temirivka and struck the R‑280 bridge near Chonhar, disrupting Russian logistics. A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone workshop in Chuguev, while a planned ballistic missile strike was cancelled. Additional Patriot missiles were sourced from expired stock and antidrone nets will be deployed in Volyn.

points: Ukrainian troops of the 67th Mechanized Brigade "Zalizni sokoly" reported successful elimination of enemy personnel near the village of Temirivka, indicating active ground engagements in that sector (DeepStateUA/23570).

Ukrainian forces conducted two precise strikes on the newly built automotive bridge of the R‑280 corridor near Chonhar, a key overland route linking Rostov‑on‑Don with Sevastopol, thereby hindering Russian logistical movements (DeepStateUA/23569).
Additional Patriot air‑defence missiles, previously slated for disposal due to expiration, were transferred to Ukrainian forces, enhancing air‑defence capability (ukr_leaks_eng/30819).
Four civilians, including a 13‑year‑old boy, were injured when a Russian attack damaged a multi‑storey building in Pavlograd, highlighting ongoing urban strike threats (UkraineNow/81833).
Ukrainian authorities plan to install antidrone nets on Volyn region roads to mitigate UAV threats (ukr_leaks_eng/30834).
A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone‑production workshop in the Chuguev area, demonstrating Russian UAV strike capability against Ukrainian military infrastructure (ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Russian officials announced the cancellation of a ballistic missile threat, indicating a de‑escalation of planned long‑range strikes (kpszsu/64818).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs over Donetsk region, reflecting continued air‑strike operations in the eastern front (kpszsu/64843).
Russian media reported development of a new pension program with employer contributions, unrelated to combat but indicating domestic policy activity (tass_agency/380212).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Donetsk, targeting Ukrainian positions (kpszsu/64843).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAVs were observed over Zaporizhzhia and Poltava regions, indicating ongoing ISR activity on the eastern axis (kpszsu/64819, eRadarrua/96197).
A Shahed‑136 type strike UAV was reported launched from the Millerevo area, showing continued use of loitering munitions in the east (eRadarrua/96150).
Ukrainian forces reported a reactive Shahed impact in the Podilskyi district of Poltava, highlighting cross‑regional UAV threats (mon1tor_ua/67564).
No explicit northern front combat reports were present in the source material.
Ukrainian strikes on the R‑280 bridge near Chonhar disrupted a critical southern supply corridor from Crimea to Russian‑occupied territories (DeepStateUA/23569).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa region, indicating pressure on southern maritime approaches (kpszsu/64821).
Russian attacks on Odesa port infrastructure threaten Ukraine's export capacity, with iron ore and over 90% of agricultural exports at risk (ukr_leaks_eng/30827).
Multiple UAV sightings were reported across Zaporizhzhia, Poltava, Dnipropetrovsk, and the Black Sea, showing high UAV traffic on both sides (kpszsu/64819, eRadarrua/96197, kpszsu/64850).
Russian Geran drone successfully destroyed a Ukrainian drone workshop in Chuguev, demonstrating offensive UAV use (ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Ukrainian forces received additional Patriot missiles, enhancing air‑defence against Russian aerial threats (ukr_leaks_eng/30819).
A ballistic missile threat was called off, reducing immediate high‑altitude strike risk (kpszsu/64818).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces eliminated enemy personnel near Temirivka, indicating active ground combat in that area (Source: @DeepStateUA/23570).
Two precise strikes disabled the automotive bridge on the R‑280 route near Chonhar, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @DeepStateUA/23569).
A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone‑production workshop in the Chuguev area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Ukraine obtained additional Patriot missiles from expired stock, bolstering its air‑defence inventory (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30819).
A previously announced ballistic missile threat was cancelled, lowering immediate long‑range strike risk (Source: @kpszsu/64818).
Antidrone nets will be installed on Volyn region roads to protect against UAV attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30834).
Four civilians were injured in a Russian strike on a multi‑storey building in Pavlograd (Source: @UkraineNow/81833).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV capabilities remain a significant threat, as evidenced by the Geran drone strike on Ukrainian infrastructure. Ukrainian ground operations are targeting critical Russian supply routes, notably the R‑280 bridge near Chonhar, which could degrade Russian logistical sustainment in the south. The cancellation of a ballistic missile threat suggests a temporary de‑escalation in high‑value strike planning, while the influx of expired Patriot missiles improves Ukraine's defensive posture against aerial attacks.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing its southern corridor defenses by striking key bridges and installing antidrone nets in the west, aiming to mitigate Russian logistical advantages. Russian forces continue to employ tactical aviation and UAVs across the eastern front, maintaining pressure on Ukrainian positions. Internationally, the risk to Odesa's port underscores the broader economic impact of sustained attacks on Ukraine's export infrastructure.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV traffic across multiple fronts should be intensified to anticipate loitering munition attacks. The effectiveness of Ukrainian bridge strikes warrants further analysis for potential replication on other Russian supply lines. The integration of expired Patriot missiles into Ukraine's air‑defence system may affect maintenance cycles and readiness, requiring logistical tracking.
